headline: Cyber Defence Centre Detects 48,000 Government Attacks
context: NACSA detected 48,000 cyberattacks on government systems in 2025 a 35% increase from previous year.

cards.big: 48,000 cyberattacks on government systems detected in 2025 up 35%
cards.sub: State-sponsored actors responsible for 12% of sophisticated intrusions

cards.big: 4 ministries suffered data breaches exposing 2.8 million citizen records
cards.sub: Health Ministry breach July 2025 compromised 1.2 million patient records

cards.big: NACSA has only 320 analysts for entire government infrastructure
cards.sub: Private sector offers 3 times salary making retention difficult

cards.big: RM580 million allocated for government cybersecurity upgrades in 2026
cards.sub: Amount covers only 40% of vulnerabilities across 800 government systems

cards.big: Government cyber defence tests whether public systems can be secured against state-level threats
48,000 government cyberattacks detected. Cyber defence at this scale needs dedicated military-grade infrastructure.
